title: cymopterus glomeratus var. glomeratus
intro: Cymopterus glomeratus var. glomeratus, also known as cluster umbel or glomerate umbel, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to the western United States and Canada. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which are attributed to its essential oils and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, such as colds, sore throats, and digestive issues, due to its health benefits, including its ability to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Cymopterus glomeratus var. glomeratus include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
